data.job_description:
Role Overview
This is a unique opportunity to join the India Shared Services Centre of one of the UK's fastest-growing private healthcare groups.
As a Financial Analyst – Corporate Finance, you will drive high-impact strategic and financial initiatives ranging from capital structuring and fundraising to M&A and growth planning. You'll work closely with the UK Head of Corporate Finance and collaborate across business units and support teams.
While this is a full-time, in-office role at our Pune Shared Services Centre, your reporting manager is based in the UK. You will therefore be expected to work with a high degree of independence, manage your own priorities, and communicate proactively with senior stakeholders across time zones.
This role is ideal for someone with a strong technical foundation in finance, a commercial mindset, and the ambition to scale with a global organisation.
Key Responsibilities
Corporate Finance & Investment Strategy
• Advise on and support external corporate finance transactions including equity and debt fundraising, property financing, and M&A.
• Conduct business case evaluations using DCF, IRR, and sensitivity models for strategic investment decisions.
• Build and maintain advanced financial models to assess capital deployment, returns, and valuation.
• Prepare investor-grade presentations and reports for the Board, investors, and senior stakeholders.
• Support exit preparedness and stakeholder communications in future financing rounds.
FP&A Support & Business Partnering
• Work closely with the FP&A team to track centre-level profitability and margin expansion initiatives.
• Act as a finance partner to UK centres, supporting cost initiatives, pricing analysis, and strategic planning.
• Assist in the development of dashboards, KPIs, and unit economics frameworks for operational improvement.
• Prepare monthly performance reviews and collaborate with clinical and commercial teams to enhance financial visibility.
Strategic Initiatives
• Contribute to cross-functional strategic projects, including service expansion, new partnerships, and geographic growth.
• Bring analytical insight to complex financial problems involving multiple stakeholders.
What Success Looks Like in Six Months
• Independently handling UK stakeholder requests.
• Delivering 2–3 high-quality financial models or analyses.
• Contributing to at least one strategic initiative (M&A / expansion / fundraising).
Skills & Experience Required
Must-Have
• 4–8 years of experience in corporate finance, corporate development, investment banking, FP&A, or consulting
• Strong command over financial modeling, valuation, and capital structuring concepts
• Excellent written and verbal communication, including ability to create polished presentations and reports
• Highly organized with strong analytical and commercial acumen
• Proven ability to manage multiple concurrent priorities and deliver under tight timelines
• Strong Excel and PowerPoint skills
• A self-starter with intellectual curiosity and a commercial mindset, comfortable operating in a fast-paced, sometimes ambiguous environment
• A CA, CA (Inter), or MBA (Finance) qualification is preferred, though not mandatory for exceptionally strong candidates.
Preferred
• Healthcare sector experience is strongly preferred
• Prior exposure to private equity, or services industries
• Hands-on experience working with UK/Europe-based clients
• Knowledge of group financial reporting, FP&A tools (e.g., Power BI, Anaplan)
Working Conditions
• Office environment in Pune (WFO).
• Standard corporate workweek, with occasional extended hours during deal closings or month-end reporting.
• Collaborative team setting; regular interaction with global finance colleagues and cross-functional business units.
Please answer the qualifying questions carefully — incomplete or casual responses may lead to your application not being considered.
If you’re ready to take ownership, sharpen your expertise, and make a real impact in global healthcare finance, apply today and grow with us!
Pay: ₹2,000,000.00 - ₹2,600,000.00 per year
Benefits:
• Health insurance
Application Question(s):
• How many total years of experience do you have in Corporate Finance, FP&A, Investment Banking, or Consulting? Please include a brief breakdown by role type.
• Please describe the most complex financial model you have built independently — including the type of model, its purpose, and your specific role in building it.
• Name the most senior audience you have presented financial analysis to. What was the key message you were communicating and what was the outcome of that presentation?
• Have you worked on any M&A, fundraising, or capital markets transactions? If yes, please describe your specific tasks and the stage of the transaction you were involved in.
• In your most recent finance role, approximately what percentage of your time involved hands-on financial modelling? Were the models built independently or in support of someone else's work?
• Are you comfortable with a full-time WFO role at our Sinhgad Road, Pune office in the UK shift (12 PM – 9 PM IST)?
• Please mention your current area of residence in Pune.
• What is your current CTC (fixed + variable, in LPA) and your expected CTC? Please note that applications without this information cannot be processed further.
• What is your notice period and your earliest possible joining date?
Work Location: In person

data.job_title: Senior Accountant - Lease Accounting and Financial Reporting
data.job_description:
Senior Accountant - Lease
The Opportunity:
Assist in the preparation of external and internal financial reporting including all periodic Securities & Exchange Commission (“SEC”) filings. Work closely with different functions including Corporate Consolidations, Global Finance, Legal, Tax, Treasury and internal and external auditors. Facilitate the information flow from all subsidiaries and the process of preparing SEC filings. Assist with improvements to financial reporting processes.This role is responsible for supporting core corporate accounting activities, including lease accounting under ASC 842, stock‑based compensation accounting under ASC 718, monthly close processes, and coordination with global teams. The position requires strong analytical abilities, attention to detail, and effective cross‑functional collaboration.
Major Responsibilities
Lease Accounting & Fixed Assets
• Enter new leases and update lease changes in the Lease Query Tool.
• Prepare and record monthly journal entries and payment reconciliations in alignment with ASC 842.
• Collaborate with global teams to support centralized lease accounting processes.
Stock‑Based Compensation
• Prepare and analyze stock‑based compensation expenses in accordance with ASC 718.
• Support quarterly disclosure requirements for 10Q/10K filings.
Month-End Close & Reporting
• Support the monthly close process, including preparation and review of journal entries, account reconciliations, analytical reviews, and documentation.
• Prepare monthly results for reporting into the data mapping tool for consolidated financials.
• Assist Financial Reporting and Consolidation teams with close activities and data needs.
Business Partnership & Collaboration
• Build strong working relationships with finance leaders and cross‑functional teams.
• Develop a solid understanding of financial processes, inter‑department data flows, reporting needs, and ongoing initiatives.
• Communicate insights clearly and responsibly to the Controller’s Group.
Audit Support
• Support quarterly internal and external audit processes with accurate, compliant documentation.
Process Improvement
• Continuously evaluate existing processes to identify opportunities to improve efficiency, accessibility of financial data, and data integrity.
Ad‑hoc Support
• Perform ad‑hoc financial and economic analyses as requested.
• Contribute to special projects and other duties as assigned.
Qualifications
Education & Experience
• CA Intermediate with 3–6 years of relevant experience, or
• CA with 1–2 years of experience in Record to Report
• Demonstrated experience with technical accounting topics such as Lease Accounting and Stock‑Based Compensation.
• Proficiency in Microsoft Excel required; experience with SAP is mandatory.
Knowledge, Skills & Abilities
• Strong understanding of U.S. GAAP, IFRS, and corporate accounting policies.
• Familiarity with Sarbanes-Oxley (SOX) requirements, with experience applying relevant controls.
• Ability to prepare, interpret, and analyze financial statements, including P&L, Balance Sheet, and Cash Flow.
• Strong written and verbal communication skills to effectively collaborate with multiple teams and stakeholders.
• Strong analytical mindset, organizational skills, and the ability to prioritize and manage multiple tasks.
• Proactive, detail‑oriented, and committed to continuous learning.

Role Overview
We are looking for a highly capable Accounting Professional to manage financial statements, lead closing activities, and handle day-to-day finance operations in SAP.
This role will function as a key support to the Financial Controller, acting as their right hand in ensuring accurate reporting, operational efficiency, and financial control across the organization.

Key Responsibilities
• Manage end-to-end financial closing activities (monthly, quarterly, and year-end)
• Prepare and review financial statements (P&L, Balance Sheet, Cash Flow) with high accuracy
• Perform daily finance and accounting operations in SAP (FICO), including postings, reconciliations, and issue resolution
• Ensure timely and accurate execution of routine accounting activities and transactions
• Act as a key support to the Financial Controller in all finance-related matters
• Assist the Controller in reporting, analysis, and decision support
• Perform account reconciliations and investigate discrepancies proactively
• Maintain and ensure integrity of financial data within SAP
• Coordinate with auditors and support internal/external audit processes
• Ensure compliance with accounting standards, internal controls, and statutory requirements
• Drive improvements in closing cycles, reporting accuracy, and finance processes

Key Requirements
• 5–10 years of experience in accounting and financial reporting
• Strong experience in managing monthly, quarterly, and year-end closings independently
• Hands-on experience and strong competencies in SAP FICO with strong exposure to daily transactional activities
• Proven ability to manage both operational accounting and reporting responsibilities
• Strong understanding of financial statements and accounting principles (IFRS/GAAP/local standards)
• Ability to work closely with senior stakeholders, especially supporting a Financial Controller
• High attention to detail with strong ownership and accountability
• Ability to work under tight deadlines and manage multiple priorities
• Strong analytical and problem-solving skills
• Excellent communication and coordination skills - fluency in english (written + spoken), conversational proficiency in German is preferred.

Preferred Qualifications
• CA / CPA / ACCA or equivalent qualification
• Experience in manufacturing or multi-entity environments
• Exposure to SAP S/4HANA
• Experience working in a fast-paced, process-driven organization

Job Description:

1. Monthly Close & Accounting Governance:

• Own and drive the monthly close calendar for India and global entities
• Review outsourced accounting entries for accuracy, completeness, and cut-off
• Ensure adherence to close timelines; escalate delays or gaps proactively
• Prepare quarterly consolidated financial statements
• Manage Accrual vs Cash P&L
• Draft and review accounting memos for non-routine / judgmental items
• Ensure adherence to transfer pricing policies
• Manage multi-entity readiness including:
• Intercompany accounting & reconciliations
• Cost allocations and cross-charges
• Consolidation preparedness (data, mappings, eliminations)
2. Reconciliations & Transaction Controls:
• Setting up the reconciliations process and own the outcome
• Track and resolve reconciling items within defined SLAs
3. Statutory, Tax & Regulatory Compliances:
• Own end-to-end compliances across India and global entities
• Ensure adherence to Tax laws, Companies Act, and payments regulations (PA-CB)
• Maintain and track a centralized compliance calendar across jurisdictions
• Ensure timely filings with zero compliance lapses or penalties
4. Audit Readiness & External Audit Management:
• Maintain continuous audit readiness (not just year-end)
• Act as the primary SPOC for auditors and drive timely closure of audit queries
5. Internal Controls & Risk Management:
• Strengthen and enforce internal control frameworks
• Support IFC/RCM documentation and testing readiness
• Drive process improvements across payments, vendors, revenue, BRS, etc.
6. Outsourced Accounting & Vendor Management:
• Coordinate with external accounting firms across geographies
• Enforce SLAs on timeliness, quality, and documentation hygiene
• Drive standardization and continuous improvement in outputs
7. Process Building & Automation:
• Design and implement scalable finance processes and internal control frameworks to support multi-entity, cross-border operations and rapid business growth
• Drive automation across accounting and finance workflows (month-end close, reconciliations, reporting, AP/AR) to improve accuracy, reduce manual dependency, and shorten closing timelines
• Evaluate, implement, and optimize finance systems and tools to enable real-time reporting, process standardization, and audit-ready documentation
8. Financial Reporting & MIS:
• Prepare monthly MIS, variance analysis, and management reporting packs
• Support budgeting and forecasting processes with data accuracy and insights
• Analyze revenue, costs, and unit economics to highlight financial risks and opportunities

• Assist in cash flow forecasting and working capital management
• Monitor liquidity across entities and optimize fund movements
• Support banking relationships and treasury documentation

• Fintech/payments industry experience preferred
• Strong ownership of month-end close, reconciliations, and audit readiness
• Experience working with outsourced accounting teams is a strong plus
• Comfortable with Zoho Books/Xero/ERP systems
• Experience in fast-growing startup environments is a plus

Ideal Candidate Profile

• Prefers review, reconciliation, validation, and sign-off over data entry
• Operates with strong close discipline: checklists, timelines, and exception tracking
• High-integrity operator who can push back on weak documentation or approvals
• Process-oriented with strong control and audit mindset
• Comfortable with fintech/payment complexities (settlements, multi-bank flows, regulatory expectations)
• Capable of managing a team and ensuring timely, accurate finance outcome

Job Description:
Ecolab is seeking an FP&A Director responsible for leading financial planning and analysis activities across the organization. This role serves as a key point of contact for Division Finance Managers and enables them to drive best‑in‑class financial performance and business insights. The position requires strong partnerships across multiple stakeholders and offers significant exposure and growth opportunities. As FP&A Director, you will work closely with divisional finance teams to drive results and strengthen financial rigor.

What’s in it for You?
• Join a fast-growing organization offering a competitive salary and benefits.
• Influence and shape your career with a company committed to continuous growth.
• Work in an environment that values diverse people, perspectives, and ideas.
• Feel proud to contribute to a company that ensures clean water, safe food, abundant energy, and healthy environments.

What You Will Do
• Lead a team of division FP&A managers in GBS+ and ensure timely completion of divisional analytics packs with robust insights explaining business performance drivers.
• Ensure delivery of services in alignment with established service-level agreements (SLAs) with divisions.
• Partner with Process Leads to drive divisional FP&A transformation initiatives.

• Lead and coordinate the development of annual plans, ensuring strong collaboration among all key stakeholders.
• Oversee monthly close and forecast processes—including providing direction, reviewing results, supporting system loads, and preparing content for senior leadership business reviews.
• Ensure teams deliver accurate, sustainable content for monthly business reviews (MBRs) and provide ad hoc financial analysis for decision‑making.
• Collaborate with broader finance and business teams to support critical reporting needs and enhance reporting capabilities and tools.

Qualifications
• Bachelor’s degree in Finance, Accounting, or Economics
• CA or equivalent professional qualification preferred
• Minimum 18 years of finance experience, with strong exposure to FP&A
• Experience in an FMCG environment strongly preferred
• Proficiency in BI tools such as Tableau or Power BI
• Strong understanding of financial and commercial reporting fundamentals
• Exceptional analytical, problem‑solving, and decision‑making skills
• Excellent communication and interpersonal skills, with the ability to influence stakeholders at all levels
• Ability to work independently and collaboratively in a fast‑paced environment

Responsibilities

Lead Analyst - Accounting

Exp - 6 - 10 years
Shift Timings - 1.30 PM - 10.30 PM

• Build, maintain, and enhance Power BI dashboards to support forecasting, performance tracking, and management reporting for SCBUs.
• Design intuitive, leadership‑friendly visualizations that highlight KPIs, financial trends, and business drivers.
• Automate manual reports and recurring processes using Power BI, Power Query, and other analytical tools to improve efficiency and reduce turnaround time.
• Collect, clean, aggregate, and validate data from multiple SCBUs and enterprise systems to ensure accuracy and reliability of reporting.
• Establish and maintain data links between different sources, building data models, and maintaining reporting datasets to support end‑to‑end dashboard operations. (Power BI datasets, Oracle Essbase/ Peoplesoft, Advanced Excel, PowerPoint, etc.) to create a seamless reporting ecosystem.
• Support forecasting, actuals reporting, and value analysis by preparing data inputs and performing first‑level variance reviews.
• Provide initial analytical support for performance metrics, and operational KPIs, highlighting anomalies and key insights.
• Assist in documenting processes, data flows, and dashboard refresh cycles.
• Highlight trends, outliers, and data inconsistencies.
• Support the SCBU FP&A team in process redesign, reporting improvements, and system enhancements to align with evolving business requirements.
• Work collaboratively with SCBU FP&A teams and cross‑BU finance partners to share best practices and ensure alignment of data and reporting standards.
• Gain a strong understanding of financial data structures, systems, and reporting tools used within the organization.
• Manage assigned deliverables independently, including prioritizing multiple tasks, meeting deadlines, and maintaining high‑quality outputs.
• Prepare clear, visually effective presentations and insights for leadership consumption.

Qualifications

Education & Experience
• Master’s degree / Professional Degree / Relevant Industry Certification
• Preferably Chartered Accountant or MBA Finance
• 6-10 years’ of relevant experience in handling complex datasets, analytics, reporting, automation & PowerBI skills. (preferably in BFSI Industry, either in GCC / service provider environment)

Technical Skills
• Strong hands‑on experience with Power BI—data modeling, DAX, dashboards, and report publishing.
• Proficiency in Oracle Essbase/ Peoplesoft, Excel (advanced formulas, pivot tables, Power Query), PowerPoint.
• Comfort working with large datasets and connecting to varied data sources.
• Basic understanding of financial statements (Income Statement, Balance Sheet).
• Experience with data cleansing, validation, and transformation.
• Exposure to SQL or other database/querying tools

Analytical & Soft Skills
• Strong problem‑solving ability and high attention to detail.
• Good communication skills with ability to translate data into insights.
• Ability to work independently, manage multiple tasks, and prioritize effectively.
• Eagerness to learn and develop deeper financial acumen.

About the Role:

The Supply Chain Risk Analyst will be responsible for identifying, assessing, and managing risks associated with third parties. The analyst will work closely with various departments and stakeholders to develop and implement effective risk management strategies. Additionally, the analyst will be responsible for understanding and mitigating Environmental, Social, and Governance (ESG) risks within the supply chain. This includes evaluating the environmental impact of suppliers, ensuring fair labor practices, and maintaining compliance with governance standards.

Responsibilities:

• Risk Assessment: Assess risks including Geographical, Reputational, Cyber Security, Sanctions, Human Resources, Business Continuity, Anti-Bribery/Anti-Corruption, Artificial Intelligence, ESG, and Privacy.
• Daily Operations: Perform day-to-day responsibilities of the SCRM program, including intake and handling of requests for new third parties, gathering documentation for due diligence and onboarding tasks, and maintaining the SCRM database.
• Monitoring: Monitor third parties for changes in their risk profile, including mergers and acquisitions, regulatory changes, and other external factors.
• Risk Assessment: Conduct risk assessments on new and existing third parties.
• Database Maintenance: Ensure the integrity of the SCRM database by accurately documenting and consistently updating contact information, products, and services to maintain reliable and trustworthy data.
• Reporting: Assist in generating standardized reports to inform business owners and management on third-party risk and performance.
• Risk Management: Encourage and enable effective risk management change.
• Support: Provide support and expertise to business owners on third-party relationship management.
• Incident Management: Conduct ongoing management of escalations and incidents related to due diligence and monitoring of third parties.
• Remediation: Facilitate or assist in the remediation of any third-party-related findings or issues.
• Program Improvement: Contribute to the improvement of the Supply Chain Risk Management Program.
• Vendor Communication: Communicate with third-party vendors to address identified risks and ensure compliance with contractual obligations and regulatory requirements.
• Industry Trends: Stay abreast of industry trends and best practices related to Supply Chain Risk Management.
• Special Projects: Assist with any special projects as needed.
• Bachelor’s degree in Information Security, Business Administration, or a related field.
• Minimum of 2 years of experience in third-party risk

Our Interview Practices

To maintain a fair and genuine hiring process, we kindly ask that all candidates participate in interviews without the assistance of AI tools or external prompts. Our interview process is designed to assess your individual skills, experiences, and communication style. We value authenticity and want to ensure we’re getting to know you—not a digital assistant. To help maintain this integrity, we ask to remove virtual backgrounds and include in-person interviews in our hiring process. Please note that use of AI-generated responses or third-party support during interviews will be grounds for disqualification from the recruitment process.

Applicants may be required to appear onsite at a Wolters Kluwer office as part of the recruitment process.

Prior experience as a Business Analyst involved in the US trading/margining systems is preferred.
